summary: The implementation addresses most requirements but has significant gaps. The backend does not implement the 2/7 class structure as requested. Instead, it uses a generic class label system without enforcing the 2-7 range. The frontend mostly implements required features including audio recording/playback, settings page, and cohesive visual theme. However, some acceptance criteria are missing or incomplete: recording quality settings are not applied (useAudioRecorder uses settings.bitrate but MediaRecorder options may not support it), storage management lacks actual enforcement of maxStorage limits, and some playback features may be incomplete. The required images (mic-icon.dim_64x64.png, play-icon.dim_64x64.png) were not provided.

missing_requirements.requirement: Backend should enforce 2/7 class structure specifically for classes 2 through 7
missing_requirements.reason: Backend accepts any text as classLabel without validation or enforcement of the 2-7 range. The class structure is not explicitly modeled as a 2/7 organizational system.
No image files were added. The implementation uses lucide-react icons instead of the requested custom minimalist icons.
Settings page includes maxStorage slider but there is no evidence of actual storage limit enforcement in the backend or frontend upload logic.

missing_requirements.requirement: Recording quality settings applied to actual recording
missing_requirements.reason: Settings includes bitrate configuration, but MediaRecorder with 'audio/webm;codecs=opus' may not respect audioBitsPerSecond parameter in all browsers. sampleRate setting is not applied to MediaRecorder.

hallucinated_features.reason: BuildRequest does not mention authentication or user management. The implementation includes a complete Internet Identity provider and authentication flow.
BuildRequest specifies warm, accessible colors but does not request dark mode support. The CSS includes complete dark mode theming.
BuildRequest does not mention auto-play functionality. Settings includes autoPlayNext toggle but it is not implemented in playback logic.
